mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy and reduced earnings growth. This mechanism suggests a negative relationship between inflation and equity returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently rises but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected. Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, which typically precedes or accompanies economic downturns. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns due to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits as economic activity slows.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicate healthy consumer demand and investment. This suggests a strong economic expansion that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns as corporate revenues and profits grow in a buoyant demand environment.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND HOUST > 1500k units
evidence_refs: fact:consumer and housing strength drives economy
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and HOUST meet the trigger conditions, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) increase the cost of capital for businesses and reduce the present value of future earnings. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors re-evaluate equity valuations downwards.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises as specified during a tightening cycle, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic activity. This translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economy expands and production capacity is utilized.
failure_condition: If INDPRO consistently shows positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a weakening economy, which typically leads to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in slower growth or recessionary conditions.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 reports negative quarter-over-quarter growth, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment (UNRATE) and strong payrolls growth (PAYEMS) often leads to inflationary pressures. This prompts central banks to tighten monetary policy, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns due to higher discount rates and reduced liquidity.
failure_condition: If UNRATE is below 5% and PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k MoM, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of such periods, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicate healthy consumer spending. This directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns as the economy benefits from strong demand.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth exceeds 0.3% MoM during a strong expansion, but S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.
